label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking which statement is true about folk medicine based on the provided passage. First, I need to go through the passage again to make sure I understand the key points about folk medicine. The passage starts by stating that folk medicine keeps the body healthy by using medicines from plants and herbs found in the surrounding environment. It mentions that since these materials are available everywhere, folk medicine can be practiced anywhere in the world. Then, the history of folk medicine is discussed, going back to the time of the earliest humans. It's noted that both humans and animals used plants and herbs for health. The example given is an animal eating snakeroot after a snake bite. The author suggests that humans probably learned to care for themselves by observing animals. Also, when animals are sick, they rest, keep quiet, and recover with the help of plants and pure air. The passage mentions that fevered animals rest in cool places, eat nothing, and drink water until the fever subsides. The last paragraph addresses some misconceptions about folk medicine. It says that some people believe folk medicine is based on old wives' tales, such as certain vegetable materials bringing good health when worn around the neck. The author dismisses this as a fairy tale and says it should be separated from the discussion of folk medicine. Now, let's consider each option: (A) It keeps one healthy by using medicines taken from plants and herbs. - This is directly stated in the first sentence of the passage. The passage says, "Folk medicine keeps the body healthy by using medicines taken from surrounding plants and herbs." So this seems true. (B) It can treat most kinds of diseases for both man and animals. - The passage mentions that folk medicine can be used by both humans and animals, but it doesn't say that it can treat most kinds of diseases. It provides examples of how animals and humans used plants for health, but there's no claim about the breadth of its effectiveness. The passage doesn't state that it can treat "most kinds of diseases," so this might be an overstatement. (C) It is better than other medicines in curing serious diseases. - The passage doesn't compare folk medicine to other types of medicine in terms of effectiveness for serious diseases. It doesn't make any claims that folk medicine is better than others, so this is likely false. (D) It was a way of body treatment used only in man's early history. - The passage says that
